Warm, but not as Warm

Last Updated Thu April 16 at 11:17AM

It takes a few days to gather and analyze all the data from various sources, but it appears we shattered record highs for the date by 3–4 degrees. Thursday is unseasonably warm, but not as warm as Wednesday. Low pressure passes to our north tonight, increasing clouds and bringing isolated showers after midnight. The west side of the Appalachians is the most likely place for those isolated showers. A bit of rain could linger along the western escarpment early Friday, but skies become mostly sunny. We return to near-record warmth Saturday ahead of a game-changing front arriving late Saturday night. We'll have widely scattered showers and thunderstorms Saturday night (mostly after midnight) and a much cooler early next week.

The RaysWeather.Com Photo Contest is underway for photos to appear in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar. We are open for submissions through April 15. We broke all records with 2026 calendar sales and look to top that with the 2027 calendar — it's our 24th calendar. Come on! Hit us with your best shot!

Get the free RWC Mobile App...: Apple and Android

Thursday
Mostly sunny; Warm, but not as warm as yesterday; West wind 5-15 mph

Thursday night
Becoming generally cloudy; Isolated showers, mostly on the west side of the Appalachians, beginning around midnight; Mild. Light wind becoming NW 5-15 mph
Hi: 76
Friday
Early clouds but becoming mostly sunny; Cannot rule out an early morning shower near the western escarpment; Pleasant temperatures. NW wind 5-15 mph
Lo: 54
Friday night
Partly to mostly cloudy; Unseasonably mild; Wind becoming calm
Hi: 81
Saturday
Morning sun, but more clouds in the afternoon; Near-record warmth; SW wind 5-15 mph
Lo: 48
Saturday night
Becoming cloudy; Mild; Widely scattered showers and thundershowers mostly after midnight; SW wind 5-15 mph, becoming NW late
Hi: 56
Sunday
Cloudy morning with widely scattered showers (thunder possible); Turning much cooler with decreasing clouds in the afternoon
Lo: 33
Sunday night
Clear; MUCH cooler; Breezy
Hi: 55
Monday
Full sunshine; Much cooler

Monday night
Clear and chilly; Areas of frost by daybreak
Tuesday

Wednesday

Extended forecast is only available for RaysWeather.com supporters.
Subscribe

Forecast Discussion

After yesterday’s record warmth, driven by high pressure off the Atlantic and a warm southwest flow, a system passing north of the area shifts us into northwest flow tonight through Friday. That brings a modest cooldown with isolated showers late tonight into early Friday morning, mainly along the west side of the Appalachians, but temperatures still run above normal for mid-April. A quick rebound arrives Saturday with another near-record warm afternoon.

Early next week brings a shift. A cold front moves through late Saturday night. We probably avoid showers during the daytime Saturday, but I cannot rule out an isolated shower or thundershower. Then widely scattered showers develop late Saturday night into Sunday morning.

Much cooler temperatures arrive Sunday and settle into the Southern Appalachians Monday. Some frost is possible in valleys Monday and Tuesday mornings.

The center of high pressure moves from the Great Lakes Monday to the Mid-Atlantic Tuesday. Tuesday afternoon will be less chilly, and Wednesday will be pleasant. We enter a more typical April pattern with seasonal temperatures and a few more opportunities for showers later next week.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
